reviews.review_description: xylophone and drums are fun, looking forward to piano mode without a piano :)

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much! Really glad you’re enjoying the xylophone and drums.

And yes - a virtual piano mode that doesn’t require a real piano is a great idea! We want people to be able to jump in and learn regardless of whether they already own instruments.

Appreciate the support and thanks for joining us this early in the journey 🙏

reviews.review_description: This is less than early access. Only 1 module available, and absolutely Nothing new in the past 6 months. The 5 star reviews are obviously from the devs. This is borderline a scam.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the honest feedback - we hear you, and you're right that the experience has been too limited with just one module.

Your timing is actually great: we just submitted a major update that introduces a fully working piano module. You can connect a real USB piano, sync it in VR, and learn lessons in a much more immersive way. It's the biggest update since launch.

We're a small studio and VR development is anything but easy, but we're putting everything into making Museverse better with every release. We'd love for you to give the new version a try, and if you have any feedback, feel free to reach out to us directly via Museverse.xyz. We read everything.

reviews.review_description:
Being a complete beginner to music, I didn't expect to love Museverse as much as I do. The app is super immersive with smooth controls which makes learning fun and intuitive. I love the Taiko drum and can't wait to play all the other instruments! 
Highly recommend✨

developer_response.body:
Wow! thank you so much for this.

Hearing that you’re a complete beginner and already loving the experience genuinely means a lot to us.

Very happy to hear the Taiko drum is hitting the spot. There’s plenty more on the way, and we can’t wait for you to explore the other instruments too.

If you ever have feedback or ideas as you continue your journey, we’re all ears. Thanks again for the recommendation - it really helps us grow 🙏

reviews.review_description:
Really enjoying Museverse so far. The music, sound design, and responsiveness already feel great. The developers are also very active and quick to respond to feedback, which makes you feel like part of the community and creative process.
As someone who plays a lot of rhythm games, I’d love to see even more challenge options and tighter timing accuracy over time, but it’s awesome to see the developers already working on improvements.
Overall, this is a really strong Early Access start with great music, solid gameplay, and a lot of potential. Looking forward to seeing how it evolves.

The first lessons on the app are really fun! I played the whole Taiko campaing and cant wait for the next campaign and instrument to be released but in the meantime I was able to play 2 more - a percussion instrument and the marimba which I would love lessons for as well.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your feedback! Lesson packs for marimba and additional instruments are already in development, so stay tuned.

reviews.review_description:
Museverse: Learn & Play Music is an enchanting and beautifully designed musical platform that lets you experiment with a variety of instruments in an immersive VR space. Even in early access, the experience feels refined and inspiring — the single giant drum alone is a joy to play, producing deep, earthy, and tribal sounds that pull you right into the rhythm.

The exercises are well thought out and help you gradually improve while staying fun and creative. I really appreciate how you can adjust your position and play style freely, making it both a learning tool and a relaxing musical playground.

Some instruments like the harp are still in development, but the upcoming update promises even more variety. A unique and uplifting VR experience for anyone who loves music or just wants to explore their inner musician!

developer_response.body: Thank you for such a beautiful review - it perfectly captures what we’re trying to create with Museverse: a place to feel music, not just play it.

reviews.review_description:
It is an early access review, Museverse is pretty cool. The visuals are 'sound' for a rythem based game and playing is pretty chilled out.

The controls are smooth, super intuitive, and it just feels good to play. It’s the kind of game where you lose track of time because you’re so caught up in the vibe. I would love some sort of feel to the drum sticks when they touch, you get a feel when striking the drums but would love if you touched sticks you had some sort of feedback.   Also it would be amazing if the views were not static and moved or you could change or add your own choice of back drop.

That said this has a lot of scope and I look forward to where the dev will take it.

developer_response.body: Fantastic feedback - thank you! We’re looking at haptics and more dynamic environments, so your suggestions are right in line with where we want to go.

reviews.review_description: Wow, I just got a chance to try this out. The drumming was really cool, great immersion with a fantastic views too. For an early access app, this has great potential, can't wait for all the instruments to come online.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for trying Museverse and sharing your thoughts! We’re really glad you enjoyed the drumming experience and the views. More instruments are on the way soon and we can’t wait to show you what’s next!

reviews.review_description:
There are so many music games on quest, but none quite like this, so relaxing and zen like.
The so incredibly realistic to real instruments, the drum at the beginning is just amazing to hit and feel like it’s real and not in VR.

I’m very early into this game and so far it’s very polished with graphics and narration.

developer_response.body: So glad you’re enjoying the instruments and atmosphere. We worked hard to make them feel as real as possible. More polish and content is on the way!

reviews.review_title: A big step for music in VR
reviews.review_description: An amazing step forward in music education and production. Immersive, inspiring, and groundbreaking – this is the future!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! We’re really excited to push VR forward in music education and creation - your encouragement means a lot as we keep building.

reviews.review_description: Everyone in the family wanted a go, including our 7 year old who said that it was great fun.

developer_response.body: Love hearing the whole family got involved - especially your 7-year-old! That’s exactly the kind of experience we hoped to create.

reviews.review_description: Thanks for all of the support with creating this app. Hope you all enjoy it!

developer_response.body: Big thanks for all your help bringing Museverse to life. We couldn’t have done it without you!

reviews.review_description:
*Early access review*

 Museverse is good rhythmic fun.
Nice relaxing setting and scenery.
Includes fun tutorial, with a weekly target chart.
You have easy, medium and hard.

Work your way through the levels, getting 1, 2 or 3 stars the better you do.

I like how you can position yourself higher or lower to the instruments, or press and use a predetermined setting based on your height.

As this was early access, at the moment there is only one tutorial for the Taiko Drum. But around you, there is other instruments  that will have tutorials added when it’s full release comes.
A couple of the instruments you can demo play on them.. and a couple are locked out fully until release.

Sounds are good, graphics and nice and clean.

Would be nice if the backgrounds had more animation or you could select your own.. maybe space, dark, an arena, stadium, audience ect..

Looking forward to seeing where the developers take this nice, neat, relaxing rhythm game

developer_response.body: Thanks for such a detailed review! More tutorials, backgrounds, and instruments are on our roadmap, so you’ll definitely see these areas expand in future updates.

reviews.review_description: Congrats with the early access launch, happy to be part of the vision, can't wait for more!

developer_response.body: Really appreciate your support from the beginning - we’ve got lots more coming in Early Access, and can’t wait to share it with you.
